hook.text: The Milky Way is a barred spiral galaxy containing 200 to 400 billion stars. We live about two-thirds of the way out.

buildup.text: It spans 100,000 light-years across. Our solar system sits in the Orion Arm, a minor spiral arm.

discovery.text: The Sun orbits the galactic center at 828,000 km/h. One full orbit takes about 230 million years.

twist.text: We can't photograph the Milky Way from outside. Every image you've seen is an artist's interpretation or another galaxy.

climax.text: Mapping our galaxy is like mapping a city while standing on one street corner. Dust blocks most of the view.

punchline.text: We live inside it but can't see its shape. Home is the hardest place to map.

quiz.question: Why can't we photograph the Milky Way from the outside?
options: We are inside it, and interstellar dust blocks much of the view
The Milky Way is invisible to all types of telescopes
Light from the Milky Way is too dim to capture
